   </ix:nonNumeric>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">During the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em>the Company granted concessions to <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c81681865">five</span> borrowers, with <em style="font: inherit;">nine</em> contracts, experiencing financial difficulties. The loans were restructured with lower interest rates or amortization periods longer than a typical loan.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">During the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2020, </em>the Company granted concessions to <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c81681867">four</span> borrowers, with <em style="font: inherit;">six</em> contracts, experiencing financial difficulties. One loan was restructured with interest rates less than a market interest rate and the remaining loans were restructured with payment deferrals.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">There were <ix:nonFraction id="c81681869" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" unitRef="Pure" name="us-gaap:FinancingReceivableModificationsSubsequentDefaultNumberOfContracts1" scale="0" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="-3">no</ix:nonFraction> TDR loans that were modified during the year ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2020</em> with a payment default. A TDR loan is considered to have payment default when it is past due <em style="font: inherit;">60</em> days or more.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">There was <em style="font: inherit;">no</em> significant financial impact from specific reserves for the TDR loans.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Section <em style="font: inherit;">4013</em> of the CARES Act, &#x201c;Temporary Relief From TDRs,&#x201d; allows financial institutions the option to temporarily suspend certain requirements under U.S. GAAP related to TDRs for a limited period of time during the COVID-<em style="font: inherit;">19</em> pandemic. This temporary suspension <em style="font: inherit;"> may </em>only be applied to modifications of loans that were <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> more than <em style="font: inherit;">30</em> days past due as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2019 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;"> may </em><em style="font: inherit;">not</em> be applied to modifications that are <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> related to the COVID-<em style="font: inherit;">19</em> pandemic. If elected, the temporary suspension <em style="font: inherit;"> may </em>be applied to eligible modifications executed during the period beginning on <em style="font: inherit;"> March 1, 2020 </em>and ending on the earlier of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2020, </em>extended to <em style="font: inherit;"> January 1, 2022 </em>under the Coronavirus Response and Relief Supplemental Appropriations Act, or <em style="font: inherit;">60</em> days after the termination of the COVID-<em style="font: inherit;">19</em> national emergency. In <em style="font: inherit;"> March 2020, </em>federal banking regulators in consultation with the FASB issued interagency statements that include similar guidance on loan modifications and reporting for financial institutions working with customers affected by COVID-<em style="font: inherit;">19.</em> The interagency statement provided that short-term modifications made on a good faith basis in response to COVID-<em style="font: inherit;">19</em> to borrowers who were current prior to any relief, are <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> to be considered TDRs.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">As of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2020, </em>the Company had <ix:nonFraction id="c81681886" contextRef="i_2020-12-31" unitRef="Pure" name="atlo:FinancingReceivableModificationsPrincipalBalanceNumberOfContracts" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">24</ix:nonFraction> COVID-<em style="font: inherit;">19</em> related loan modifications still in the modification period with a total outstanding principal balance of $<ix:nonFraction id="c81681888" contextRef="i_2020-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="atlo:FinancingReceivableModificationsPrincipalBalance" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">45.9</ix:nonFraction> million. As of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em>substantially all of the COVID-<em style="font: inherit;">19</em> related loan modifications have returned to normal payment status.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <ix:exclude>
    <div class="PGNUM" style="text-align: right; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: Times New Roman;">
     <em style="font: inherit;">75</em>
    </div>

   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">There are <em style="font: inherit;">no</em> other known problem loans that cause management to have serious doubts as to the ability of such borrowers to comply with the present loan repayment terms.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">As of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em>there were <ix:nonFraction id="c81681893" contextRef="i_2021-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:LoansAndLeasesReceivableImpairedCommitmentToLend" scale="0" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="-3">no</ix:nonFraction> material commitments to lend additional funds to customers whose loans were classified as impaired.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Loans are made in the normal course of business to certain directors and executive officers of the Company and to their affiliates. The terms of these loans, including interest rates and collateral, are similar to those prevailing for comparable transactions with others and do <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> involve more than a normal risk of collectability.   <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" name="us-gaap:GoodwillDisclosureTextBlock" id="c2052976" escape="true">
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt 0pt 0pt 5pt;text-indent:-5pt;"><b>Note <em style="font: inherit;">6.</em> Goodwill</b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Goodwill arose in connection with <em style="font: inherit;">four</em> acquisitions in previous periods. Accounting standards allow for goodwill to be tested for impairment by performing a qualitative assessment to determine whether it is more likely than <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> that the fair value of the reporting unit is less than its carrying value. There was <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c81682640"><span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c81682641">no</span></span> impairment of the carrying amount of goodwill in <em style="font: inherit;">2021</em> and <em style="font: inherit;">2020.</em></p>
  </ix:nonNumeric>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <div>
    &#xa0;
  </div>
  <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" name="us-gaap:IntangibleAssetsDisclosureTextBlock" id="c2052977" escape="true">
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt 0pt 0pt 5pt;text-indent:-5pt;"><b>Note <em style="font: inherit;">7.</em> Intangible Assets</b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>

   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Borrowed funds at <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2020</em> consisted of an FHLB advance. FHLB advances are collateralized by FHLB stock, certain <em style="font: inherit;">1</em>-<em style="font: inherit;">4</em> family residential real estate loans, multifamily real estate loans, commercial real estate loans and agricultural real estate loans. The Banks had available borrowing capacity with the FHLB of Des Moines, Iowa of $<ix:nonFraction id="c81682728" contextRef="i_2021-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:FederalHomeLoanBankAdvancesGeneralDebtObligationsDisclosuresAmountOfAvailableUnusedFunds" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">309.4</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction id="c81682729" contextRef="i_2020-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:FederalHomeLoanBankAdvancesGeneralDebtObligationsDisclosuresAmountOfAvailableUnusedFunds" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-6">208.0</ix:nonFraction> million at <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> respectively.</p>

   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;"><b>Note <em style="font: inherit;">11.</em> Employee Benefit Plans</b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">The Company has a qualified <em style="font: inherit;">401</em>(k) profit-sharing plan. For the years ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> the Company matched employee contributions up to a maximum of <ix:nonFraction id="c81682739" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" unitRef="Pure" name="us-gaap:DefinedContributionPlanEmployerMatchingContributionPercent" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="2">3</ix:nonFraction>% and contributed an amount equal to <ix:nonFraction id="c81682741" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" unitRef="Pure" name="us-gaap:DefinedContributionPlanMaximumAnnualContributionsPerEmployeePercent" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="2">3</ix:nonFraction>% of the participating employee&#x2019;s compensation. For the years ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> Company contributions to the plan were approximately $<ix:nonFraction id="c81682744" contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:DefinedContributionPlanEmployerDiscretionaryContributionAmount" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-6">1.0</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction id="c81682745" contextRef="d_2020-01-01_2020-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:DefinedContributionPlanEmployerDiscretionaryContributionAmount" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">1.1</ix:nonFraction> million, respectively. The plan covers substantially all employees.</p>
  </ix:nonNumeric>

   </ix:nonNumeric>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">The Company has approximately $<ix:nonFraction id="c81682753" contextRef="i_2021-12-31_IncomeTaxAuthorityAxis-StateAndLocalJurisdictionMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:OperatingLossCarryforwards" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">396</ix:nonFraction> thousand of state income taxes associated with state net operating loss (&#x201c;NOL&#x201d;) carryforwards as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021. </em>In <em style="font: inherit;">2021,</em> the Company has recorded a valuation allowance against the tax effect of the NOL in the amount of $<ix:nonFraction id="c81682755" contextRef="i_2021-12-31_IncomeTaxAuthorityAxis-StateAndLocalJurisdictionMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:OperatingLossCarryforwardsValuationAllowance" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">396</ix:nonFraction> thousand, as management believes it is more likely than <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> that such carryforwards will <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> be utilized.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">The Company has approximately $<ix:nonFraction id="c81682758" contextRef="i_2021-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:DeferredTaxAssetsTaxCreditCarryforwardsAlternativeMinimumTax" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">226</ix:nonFraction> thousand of state alternative minimum tax (&#x201c;AMT&#x201d;) credit carryforwards available to offset future state alternative minimum taxable income as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2020.</em> The Company has recorded a valuation allowance against the tax effect of the AMT credit carryforwards, as management believes it is more likely than <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> that such carryforwards will <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> be utilized.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">The Company and its subsidiaries file <em style="font: inherit;">one</em> income tax return in the U.S. federal jurisdiction and separate tax returns for the state of Iowa. The Company is <em style="font: inherit;">no</em> longer subject to U.S. federal income and state tax examinations for years before <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c81682765">2018.</span></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">The Company follows the accounting requirements for uncertain tax positions. Management has determined that the Company has <ix:nonFraction id="c81682766" contextRef="i_2021-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:LiabilityForUncertainTaxPositionsCurrent" scale="0" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="-3">no</ix:nonFraction> material uncertain tax positions and <ix:nonFraction id="c81682768" contextRef="i_2021-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:IncomeTaxExaminationPenaltiesAndInterestAccrued" scale="0" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="-3">no</ix:nonFraction> material accrued interest or penalties as of or for the years ended <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2020</em> that would require recognition. The Company had <ix:nonFraction id="c81682771" contextRef="i_2021-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:UnrecognizedTaxBenefits" scale="0" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="-3">no</ix:nonFraction> significant unrecognized tax benefits as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em>that if recognized, would affect the effective tax rate. The Company had <ix:nonFraction id="c81682772" contextRef="i_2021-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:SignificantChangeInUnrecognizedTaxBenefitsIsReasonablyPossibleAmountOfUnrecordedBenefit" scale="0" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="-3">no</ix:nonFraction> positions for which it deemed that it is reasonably possible that the total amounts of the unrecognized tax benefit will significantly increase or decrease within the next <em style="font: inherit;">12</em> months as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2020.</em></p>
  </ix:nonNumeric>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>

  <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" name="us-gaap:CommitmentsAndContingenciesDisclosureTextBlock" id="c2052994" escape="true">
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;"><b>Note <em style="font: inherit;">13.</em> Commitments, Contingencies and Concentrations of Credit Risk</b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">The Company is party to financial instruments with off-balance-sheet risk in the normal course of business. These financial instruments include commitments to extend credit and standby letters of credit. These instruments involve, to varying degrees, elements of credit risk in excess of the amount recognized in the balance sheet.</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <ix:exclude>
    <div class="PGNUM" style="text-align: right; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: Times New Roman;">
     <em style="font: inherit;">81</em>
    </div>

   <ix:exclude>&#xa0;</ix:exclude>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">The Company&#x2019;s exposure to credit loss in the event of nonperformance by the other party to the financial instrument for commitments to extend credit and standby letters of credit is represented by the contractual amount of those instruments. The Company uses the same credit policies in making commitments and conditional obligations as they do for on-balance-sheet instruments. A summary of the Company&#x2019;s commitments as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2020</em> is as follows <i>(in thousands)</i>:</p>

<tr style="vertical-align: bottom; background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);"><td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;"> <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">Standby letters of credit</p> </td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0);">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 14%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0); text-align: right;"><ix:nonFraction id="c81682870" contextRef="i_2021-12-31_FinancialInstrumentAxis-StandbyLettersOfCreditMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:FairValueDisclosureOffbalanceSheetRisksAmountLiability" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-4">6,540</ix:nonFraction></td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0); margin-left: 0pt;">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0);">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0);">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 14%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0); text-align: right;"><ix:nonFraction id="c81682871" contextRef="i_2020-12-31_FinancialInstrumentAxis-StandbyLettersOfCreditMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:FairValueDisclosureOffbalanceSheetRisksAmountLiability" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">7,168</ix:nonFraction></td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; padding-bottom: 1px; margin-left: 0pt;">&#xa0;</td></tr>

</tbody></table>
   </ix:nonNumeric>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Commitments to extend credit are agreements to lend to a customer if there is <em style="font: inherit;">no</em> violation of any condition established in the contract. As of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> approximately $<ix:nonFraction id="c81682855" contextRef="i_2021-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="atlo:FixedInterestRateCommitmentsToExtendCredit" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">129.8</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction id="c81682856" contextRef="i_2020-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="atlo:FixedInterestRateCommitmentsToExtendCredit" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">131.8</ix:nonFraction> million of the commitments to extend credit were fixed interest rates. Since many of the commitments are expected to expire without being drawn upon, the total commitment amounts do <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> necessarily represent future cash requirements. The Banks evaluate each customer&#x2019;s creditworthiness on a case-by-case basis. The amount of collateral obtained, if deemed necessary by the Banks upon extension of credit, is based on management&#x2019;s credit evaluation of the party.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Standby letters of credit are conditional commitments issued by the Banks to guarantee the performance of a customer to a <em style="font: inherit;">third</em>-party. Those guarantees are primarily issued to support public and private borrowing arrangements. The credit risk involved in issuing letters of credit is essentially the same as that involved in extending loan facilities to customers. Collateral held varies and is required in instances which the Banks deem necessary. In the event the customer does <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> perform in accordance with the terms of the agreement with the <em style="font: inherit;">third</em> party, the Banks would be required to fund the commitment. The maximum potential amount of future payments the Banks could be required to make is represented by the contractual amount shown in the summary above. If the commitments were funded, the Banks would be entitled to seek recovery from the customer.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">As of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> the Banks have established liabilities totaling approximately $<ix:nonFraction id="c81682862" contextRef="i_2021-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="atlo:LiabilitiesToCoverCreditLossesForOffBalanceSheet" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">692</ix:nonFraction> thousand and $<ix:nonFraction id="c81682863" contextRef="i_2020-12-31" unitRef="USD" name="atlo:LiabilitiesToCoverCreditLossesForOffBalanceSheet" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3">713</ix:nonFraction> thousand, respectively to cover estimated credit losses for off-balance-sheet loan commitments and standby letters of credit.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">On <em style="font: inherit;"> April 16, 2021, </em>the Company entered into a commitment with a contractor to build a new branch in West Des Moines, Iowa for $<ix:nonFraction id="c81682864" contextRef="i_2021-04-16_OtherCommitmentsAxis-CommitmentToBuildNewBranchInWestDesMoinesIowaMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:OtherCommitment" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">1.8</ix:nonFraction> million. There was $<ix:nonFraction id="c81682865" contextRef="i_2021-12-31_OtherCommitmentsAxis-CommitmentToBuildNewBranchInWestDesMoinesIowaMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:OtherCommitment" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-6">1.0</ix:nonFraction> million remaining on the commitment as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021.</em></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">In the normal course of business, the Company is involved in various legal proceedings. In the opinion of management, any liability resulting from such proceedings would <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> have a material adverse effect on the Company&#x2019;s financial statements.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Concentrations of credit risk: The Banks originate real estate, consumer, and commercial loans, primarily in Boone, Clarke, Hancock, Marshall, Polk, Story and Union counties in Iowa, as well as adjacent counties. Although the Banks have diversified loan portfolios, a substantial portion of their borrowers&#x2019; ability to repay loans is dependent upon economic conditions in the Banks&#x2019; market areas.</p>
  </ix:nonNumeric>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <div>
    &#xa0;
  </div>
  <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" name="us-gaap:RegulatoryCapitalRequirementsUnderBankingRegulationsTextBlock" id="c2052996" escape="true">
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;"><b>Note <em style="font: inherit;">14.</em> Regulatory Matters</b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">The Company and the Banks are subject to various regulatory capital requirements administered by the federal banking agencies. Failure to meet minimum capital requirements can initiate certain mandatory and possible additional discretionary actions by regulators that, if undertaken, could have a direct material effect on the Company&#x2019;s and Banks&#x2019; financial statements. Under capital adequacy guidelines and the regulatory framework for prompt corrective action, the Banks must meet specific capital guidelines that involve quantitative measures of their assets, liabilities and certain off-balance-sheet items as calculated under regulatory accounting practices. The capital amounts and classification are also subject to qualitative judgments by the regulators about components, risk weightings, and other factors. Regulators also have the ability to impose higher limits than those specified by capital adequacy guidelines if they so deem necessary. Management believes, as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> that the Company and each subsidiary bank met all capital adequacy requirements to which they are subject.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>

font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 11%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; text-align: right;"><ix:nonFraction id="c81683122" contextRef="i_2020-12-31_LegalEntityAxis-UnitedBankAndTrustMember" unitRef="Pure" name="us-gaap:TierOneLeverageCapitalToAverageAssets" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="3">9.2</ix:nonFraction></td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 11%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; text-align: right;"><ix:nonFraction id="c81683123" contextRef="i_2020-12-31_LegalEntityAxis-UnitedBankAndTrustMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:TierOneLeverageCapitalRequiredToBeWellCapitalized" scale="3" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-4">9,180</ix:nonFraction></td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 11%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;"><ix:nonFraction id="c81683124" contextRef="i_2020-12-31_LegalEntityAxis-UnitedBankAndTrustMember" unitRef="Pure" name="us-gaap:TierOneLeverageCapitalRequiredToBeWellCapitalizedToAverageAssets" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="2">8.0</ix:nonFraction></td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">&#xa0;</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
   </ix:continuation>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">The Company and the Banks are subject to the rules of the Basel III regulatory capital framework and related Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act. The rules include the implementation of a <ix:nonFraction id="c81682881" contextRef="i_2021-12-31" unitRef="Pure" name="us-gaap:BankingRegulationCapitalConservationBufferTotalRiskBasedCapitalActual" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="3">2.5</ix:nonFraction> percent capital conservation buffer that is added to the minimum requirements for capital adequacy purposes. A banking organization with a capital conservation buffer of less than the required amount will be subject to limitations on capital distributions, including dividend payments, and certain discretionary bonus payments to executive officers. At <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em>the capital ratios for the Company and the Banks were sufficient to meet the conservation buffer.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">On <em style="font: inherit;"> March 31, 2020, </em>the Banks qualified for the community bank leverage ratio (CBLR) framework. In order to qualify for the CBLR framework, a community banking organization must have a tier <em style="font: inherit;">1</em> leverage ratio of greater than <em style="font: inherit;">9</em> percent, less than <em style="font: inherit;">$10</em> billion in total consolidated assets, and limited amounts of off-balance-sheet exposures and trading assets and liabilities. The CARES ACT lowered the CBLR to <em style="font: inherit;">8%</em> beginning in the <em style="font: inherit;">second</em> quarter of <em style="font: inherit;">2020.</em> Beginning in <em style="font: inherit;">2021,</em> the CBLR was increased to <em style="font: inherit;">8.5%</em> for the calendar year and will increase to <em style="font: inherit;">9%</em> beginning <em style="font: inherit;"> January 1, 2022. </em>A qualifying community banking organization that opts into the CBLR framework and meets all requirements under the framework will be considered to have met the well capitalized ratio requirements under the Prompt Corrective Action regulations and will <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> be required to report or calculate risk-based capital. The Banks elected to stop reporting their capital ratios under the Community Bank Leverage Ratio as of <em style="font: inherit;"> September 30, 2021.</em></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Federal and state banking regulations place certain restrictions on dividends paid and loans or advances made by the Banks to the Company. Dividends paid by each Bank to the Company would be prohibited if the effect thereof would cause the Bank&#x2019;s capital to be reduced below applicable minimum capital requirements. Management believes that these restrictions currently do <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> have a significant impact on the Company.</p>
  </ix:nonNumeric>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <div class="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
   <div class="PGNUM" style="text-align: right; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: Times New Roman;">
     84
   </div>
   <hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000" />
   <div class="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%">
    <div class="hf-row">
     <div class="TOCLink hf-cell" style="width: 100%; display: inline-block; text-align: left;">
      <a href="#toc" style="font-weight: initial; font-style: initial; font-variant: initial; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif;">Table of Contents</a>
     </div>
    </div>

  </div>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <div>
    &#xa0;
  </div>
  <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" name="us-gaap:FairValueDisclosuresTextBlock" id="c2052999" escape="true">
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;"><b>Note <em style="font: inherit;">15.</em> Fair Value Measurements</b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Fair value is defined as the price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability in an orderly transaction between market participants. A fair value measurement assumes that the transaction to sell the asset or transfer the liability occurs in the principal market for the asset or liability or, in the absence of a principal market, the most advantageous market for the asset or liability. The price in the principal (or most advantageous) market used to measure the fair value of the asset or liability shall <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> be adjusted for transaction costs. An orderly transaction is a transaction that assumes exposure to the market for a period prior to the measurement date to allow for marketing activities that are usual and customary for transactions involving such assets and liabilities; it is <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> a forced transaction. Market participants are buyers and sellers in the principal market that are (i) independent, (ii) knowledgeable, (iii) able to transact, and (iv) willing to transact.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">The standards require the use of valuation techniques that are consistent with the market approach, the income approach, and/or the cost approach. The market approach uses prices and other relevant information generated by market transactions involving identical or comparable assets and liabilities. The income approach uses valuation techniques to convert future amounts, such as cash flows or earnings, to a single present amount on a discounted basis. The cost approach is based on the amount that currently would be required to replace the service capacity of an asset (replacement cost). Valuation techniques are consistently applied. Inputs to valuation techniques refer to the assumptions that market participants would use in pricing the asset or liability. Inputs <em style="font: inherit;"> may </em>be observable, meaning those that reflect the assumptions market participants would use in pricing the asset or liability developed based on market data obtained from independent sources, or unobservable, meaning those that reflect the Company&#x2019;s own assumptions about the assumptions market participants would use in pricing the asset or liability developed based on the best information available in the circumstances. In that regard, a fair value hierarchy was established for valuation inputs that gives the highest priority to quoted prices in active markets for identical assets or liabilities and the lowest priority to unobservable inputs. The fair value hierarchy is as follows:</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Level <em style="font: inherit;">1:</em>&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0; Inputs to the valuation methodology are quoted prices, unadjusted, for identical assets or liabilities in active markets. A quoted price in an active market provides the most reliable evidence of fair value and shall be used to measure fair value whenever available.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Level <em style="font: inherit;">2:</em>&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0; Inputs to the valuation methodology include: quoted prices for similar assets or liabilities in active markets; quoted process for identical or similar assets or liabilities in markets that are <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> active; inputs other than quoted prices that are observable for the asset or liability (such as interest rates, volatility, prepayment speeds, credit risk); or inputs derived principally from or can be corroborated by observable market data by correlation or other means.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Level <em style="font: inherit;">3:</em>&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0; Inputs to the valuation methodology are unobservable and significant to the fair value measurement. Level <em style="font: inherit;">3</em> assets and liabilities include financial instruments whose value is determined using discounted cash flow methodologies, as well as instruments for which the determination of fair value requires significant management judgment or estimation.</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <ix:exclude>

   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>

   </ix:nonNumeric>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Level <em style="font: inherit;">1</em> securities include U.S. Treasury securities that are traded by dealers or brokers in active over-the-counter markets. U.S. government agencies, mortgage-backed securities, state and political subdivisions, and most corporate bonds are reported at fair value utilizing Level <em style="font: inherit;">2</em> inputs. For these securities, the Company obtains fair value measurements from an independent pricing service. The fair value measurements consider observable data that <em style="font: inherit;"> may </em>include dealer quotes, market spreads, cash flows, the U.S. Treasury yield curve, live trading levels, trade execution data, market consensus prepayment speeds, credit information and the security&#x2019;s terms and conditions, among other things.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">The Company reviews the prices supplied by the independent pricing service, as well as their underlying pricing methodologies, for reasonableness and to ensure such prices are aligned with traditional pricing matrices. In general, the Company does <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> purchase investment portfolio securities that are esoteric or that have a complicated structure. The Company&#x2019;s entire portfolio consists of traditional investments, nearly all of which are federal agency or mortgage pass-through securities, general obligation or revenue based municipal bonds and corporate bonds. Annually, the Company will validate prices supplied by the independent pricing service by comparison to prices obtained from <em style="font: inherit;">third</em>-party sources.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <ix:exclude>
    <div class="PGNUM" style="text-align: right; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: Times New Roman;">
     <em style="font: inherit;">86</em>
    </div>

   </ix:exclude>
   <ix:exclude>&#xa0;</ix:exclude>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Certain assets are measured at fair value on a nonrecurring basis; that is, they are subject to fair value adjustments in certain circumstances (for example, when there is evidence of impairment or a change in previously recognized impairment). The following table presents the assets carried on the balance sheet (after specific reserves) by caption and by level with the valuation hierarchy as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2020</em> <i>(in thousands)</i>:</p>

   </ix:continuation>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">* <em style="font: inherit;">Not</em> Meaningful.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Evaluations of the underlying assets are completed for each impaired collateral dependent loan with a specific reserve. The types of collateral vary widely and could include accounts receivables, inventory, a variety of equipment and real estate. Collateral evaluations are reviewed and discounted as appropriate based on knowledge of the specific type of collateral. In the case of real estate, an independent appraisal <em style="font: inherit;"> may </em>be obtained. Types of discounts considered included aging of receivables, condition of the collateral, potential market for the collateral and estimated disposal costs. These discounts will vary from loan to loan, thus providing a range would <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> be meaningful.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <ix:exclude>

     <em style="font: inherit;">87</em>

   </ix:exclude>
   <ix:exclude>&#xa0;</ix:exclude>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">GAAP requires disclosure of the fair value of financial assets and financial liabilities, including those that are <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> measured and reported at fair value on a recurring basis or nonrecurring basis. The following table includes the carrying amounts and estimated fair values of financial assets and liabilities as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2020</em> <i>(in thousands)</i>:</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" name="us-gaap:FairValueByBalanceSheetGroupingTextBlock" id="c2053004" escape="true">

   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;"><span style="text-decoration: underline; ">Commitments to extend credit and standby letters of credit</span>: The fair values of commitments to extend credit and standby letters of credit are based on fees currently charged to enter into similar agreements, considering the remaining terms of the agreement and credit worthiness of the counterparties. The carrying value and fair value of the commitments to extend credit and standby letters of credit are <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> considered significant.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;"><span style="text-decoration: underline; ">Limitations</span>: Fair value estimates are made at a specific point in time, based on relevant market information and information about the financial instrument. Because <em style="font: inherit;">no</em> market exists for a significant portion of the Company&#x2019;s financial instruments, fair value estimates are based on judgments regarding future expected loss experience, current economic conditions, risk characteristics of various financial instruments, and other factors. These estimates are subjective in nature and involve uncertainties and matters of significant judgment and, therefore, cannot be determined with precision. Changes in assumptions could significantly affect the estimates.</p>
  </ix:nonNumeric>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>

   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;"><b>Note <em style="font: inherit;">16.</em> Subsequent Events</b></p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Management evaluated subsequent events through the date the financial statements were issued. There were <em style="font: inherit;">no</em> significant events or transactions occurring after <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021, </em>but prior to <em style="font: inherit;"> March 11, 2022, </em>that provided additional evidence about conditions that existed as of <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021. </em>There were <em style="font: inherit;">no</em> significant events or transactions that provided evidence about conditions that did <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> exist at <em style="font: inherit;"> December 31, 2021.</em></p>
  </ix:nonNumeric>

  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;"><b><a id="a9" title="9" href="#"></a>ITEM 9. CHANGES IN AND DISAGREEMENTS WITH ACCOUNTANTS ON ACCOUNTING AND FINANCIAL DISCLOSURE </b></p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">None.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;"><b><a id="a9a" title="9a" href="#"></a>ITEM 9A. CONTROLS AND PROCEDURES</b></p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;"><b>Disclosure Controls and Procedures</b></p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">As of the end of the period covered by this report, an evaluation of the effectiveness of the Company&#x2019;s disclosure controls and procedures (as defined in Exchange Act Rule 13a-15(e)) was performed under the supervision and with the participation of the Company&#x2019;s Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer. Based on that evaluation, the Chief Executive Officer and the Chief Financial Officer have concluded that the Company&#x2019;s current disclosure controls and procedures were effective to ensure that information required to be disclosed by the Company in the reports that it files or submits under the Exchange Act was recorded, processed, summarized and reported within the time periods specified in the SEC&#x2019;s rules and forms.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;"><b>Management</b>&#x2019;<b>s Report on Internal Control over Financial Reporting</b></p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Management of the Company is responsible for establishing and maintaining adequate internal control over financial reporting (as defined in Rules 13a-15(f) and 15d-15(f) under the Exchange Act). The Company&#x2019;s internal control system is a process designed to provide reasonable assurance to the Company&#x2019;s management and Board of Directors regarding the preparation and fair presentation of published financial statements.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Internal control over financial reporting of the Company includes those policies and procedures that pertain to the maintenance of records that, in reasonable detail, accurately and fairly reflect the transactions of the Company; provide reasonable assurance that transactions are recorded as necessary to permit preparation of financial statements in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles, and that receipts and expenditures of the Company are being made only in accordance with authorizations of management and directors of the Company; and provide reasonable assurance regarding prevention or timely detection of unauthorized acquisition, use or disposition of the Company&#x2019;s assets that could have a material effect on the Company&#x2019;s consolidated financial statements.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Because of inherent limitations in any system of internal control, no matter how well designed, misstatements due to error or fraud may occur and not be detected, including the possibility of the circumvention or overriding of controls. Accordingly, even effective internal control over financial reporting can provide only reasonable assurance with respect to financial statement preparation. Further, because of changes in conditions, internal control effectiveness may vary over time.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Management assessed the Company&#x2019;s internal control over financial reporting as of December 31, 2021. This assessment was based on criteria for effective internal control over financial reporting set forth by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations of the Treadway Commission in Internal Control-Integrated Framework in 2013. Based on this assessment, the Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer have concluded that the Company maintained effective internal control over financial reporting as of December 31, 2021 based on the specified criteria.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;"><b>Changes in Internal Control Over Financial Reporting</b></p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">There were no changes in the Company&#x2019;s internal control over financial reporting (as defined in Rules 13a-15(f) and 15d-15(f) under the Exchange Act) that occurred during the quarter ended December 31, 2021 that have materially affected, or are reasonably likely to materially affect, the Company&#x2019;s internal control over financial reporting.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;"><b><a id="a9b" title="9b" href="#"></a>ITEM 9B. OTHER INFORMATION</b></p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">None.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;"><b><a id="a9c" title="9c" href="#"></a>ITEM 9C. DISCLOSURE REGARDING FOREIGN JURISDICTIONS THAT PREVENT INSPECTIONS</b></p>

  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;"><b><a id="a10" title="10" href="#"></a>ITEM 10. DIRECTORS, EXECUTIVE OFFICERS AND CORPORATE GOVERANCE</b></p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">Directors</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Refer to the information under the captions &#x201c;Corporate Governance&#x201d; and "Proposals to be Voted on at Meeting &#x2013; Proposal 1 &#x2013; Election of Directors&#x201d; contained in the Company's definitive proxy statement prepared in connection with its Annual Meeting of Shareholders to be held April 27, 2022, as filed with the SEC on March 11, 2022 (the "Proxy Statement"), which information is incorporated herein by this reference.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Executive Officers</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">The information regarding the executive officers appears in Item 1 of Part I of this Annual Report under the heading &#x201c;Information About our Executive Officers&#x201d;.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Section 16(a) Beneficial Ownership Reporting Compliance</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Refer to the information under the caption &#x201c;Delinquent Section 16(a) Reports&#x201d; in the Proxy Statement, which information is incorporated herein by this reference.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Audit Committee</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">The Company has established an Audit Committee as a standing committee of the Board of Directors. Refer to the information under the caption &#x201c;Corporate Governance &#x2013; Board Committees&#x201d; in the Proxy Statement, which information is incorporated herein by this reference.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Audit Committee Financial Expert</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">The Board of Directors of the Company has determined that Lisa M. Eslinger, a director and member of the Audit Committee, qualifies as an "audit committee financial expert" under applicable SEC rules. The Board of Directors has further determined that Ms. Eslinger qualifies as an "independent" director under applicable SEC rules and the corporate governance rules of the NASDAQ stock market. The Board's affirmative determination was based, among other things, upon Ms. Eslinger's experience as Chief Financial and Administrative Officer for the Iowa State Foundation. Prior to joining the foundation, Ms. Eslinger was a senior manager with KPMG LLP.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">Code of Ethics</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">The Company has adopted an Ethics and Confidentiality Policy that applies to all directors, officers and employees of the Company, including the Chief Executive Officer and the Chief Financial Officer of the Company. A copy of this policy is posted on the Company's website at <span style="text-decoration: underline; ">www.amesnational.com</span>. In the event that the Company makes any amendments to, or grants any waivers of, a provision of the Ethics and Confidentiality Policy that requires disclosure under applicable SEC rules, the Company intends to disclose such amendments or waiver and the reasons therefor on its website.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;"><b><a id="a11" title="11" href="#"></a>ITEM 11. EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION</b></p>

  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt 0pt 0pt 54pt;text-indent:-54pt;"><b><a id="a12" title="12" href="#"></a>ITEM 12. SECURITY OWNERSHIP OF CERTAIN BENEFICIAL OWNERS AND MANAGEMENT AND RELATED SHAREHOLDER MATTERS</b></p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;text-indent:9pt;">&#xa0;</p>

The Company does not maintain any equity compensation plans covering its directors, officers or employees or the directors, officers or employees of the Banks.</p>

  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt 0pt 0pt 54pt;text-indent:-54pt;"><b><a id="a13" title="13" href="#"></a>ITEM 13. CERTAIN RELATIONSHIPS AND RELATED TRANSACTIONS AND DIRECTOR INDEPENDENCE</b></p>

  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;"><b><a id="a14" title="14" href="#"></a>ITEM 14. PRINCIPAL ACCOUNTANT FEES AND SERVICES</b></p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: 10pt; font-variant: normal; margin: 0pt;">Our independent registered public accounting firm is <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" name="dei:AuditorName">CliftonLarsonAllen LLP</ix:nonNumeric>, <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" name="dei:AuditorLocation">West Des Moines, IA.</ix:nonNumeric>, PCAOB ID: <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2021-01-01_2021-12-31" name="dei:AuditorFirmId">655</ix:nonNumeric>.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman';font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;margin:0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:Times New Roman;font-size:10pt;font-variant:normal;text-align:justify;margin:0pt;">Refer to the information under the caption "Relationship with Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm" in the Proxy Statement, which information is incorporated herein by this reference.</p>
